trajectory_summary: This call produced no interaction. The dialer connected to a voicemail system; the transcript captures only the outgoing greeting with zero rep speech and zero customer engagement. There is no trajectory to evaluate — no energy was built, leaked, or stagnated because no call occurred. This record should be removed from the coaching queue and routed to operations for logging review.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is 'unknown,' which is technically accurate but undersells the operational issue. The correct classification is 'voicemail / no contact' — this is a dialer or logging artifact, not a setter call. It should not appear in any coaching or performance analysis for the rep.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Ops routing — null-interaction records should not reach the coaching queue
primary_coaching_focus.quote: You've reached Steve's voice mail. I can't get to the phone right now, but leave me a message. I'll get back to you as soon as I can. Thanks. Bye.
primary_coaching_focus.why: There is no rep behavior to coach here. The only productive use of this record is as a trigger to fix the logging or AMD filter that allowed it to enter the review queue. Every minute a manager spends on a null-interaction transcript is a minute not spent on a call with actual coachable content.

key_moments.quote: You've reached Steve's voice mail. I can't get to the phone right now, but leave me a message. I'll get back to you as soon as I can. Thanks. Bye.
key_moments.stewart_read: The call record contains only a voicemail greeting. No rep speech was captured. This is a null-interaction event — there is no setter behavior to evaluate, positively or negatively.

key_moments.quote: You've reached Steve's voice mail. I can't get to the phone right now, but leave me a message. I'll get back to you as soon as I can. Thanks. Bye.
key_moments.stewart_read: The voicemail greeting belongs to 'Steve.' If the lead record for this call_id is assigned to a different customer name, that is a dialer routing error — the wrong number was dialed. If the lead record does show 'Steve,' the call was correctly dialed but not answered, and AMD (answering machine detection) either failed or was not configured to suppress this record from the transcript queue.

key_moments.quote: You've reached Steve's voice mail. I can't get to the phone right now, but leave me a message. I'll get back to you as soon as I can. Thanks. Bye.
key_moments.stewart_read: If the rep did manually dial this number outside the dialer system and reached voicemail, the transcript would look identical to what is recorded here. However, there is no evidence in this transcript to support that hypothesis — it is equally consistent with a standard dialer voicemail drop. No inference about rep behavior outside the dialer is warranted from this record alone.

key_moments.quote: You've reached Steve's voice mail. I can't get to the phone right now, but leave me a message. I'll get back to you as soon as I can. Thanks. Bye.
key_moments.stewart_read: This record reaching the coaching transcript queue at all is the only actionable signal in the call. If Stewart is processing a material volume of null-interaction records like this one, the manager is spending review time on calls that contain no coachable content — a direct tax on the 30-minute Tuesday review window.
